description: Lookup table for classifying codes as administrative vs. medical/pharmacy. Codes listed here will be filtered out during event filtering (Step 1b). For falls analysis: Z91.81 (fall history) and CPT 1100F (falls risk screening) are excluded as they represent history/screening rather than actual fall events.
documentation: Falls target: R29.6, T02.*, S12.*, S22.*, S32.*, S42.*, S52.*, S62.*, S72.*, S82.*, S92.*. Excluded: Z91.81 (personal history of falls — administrative/historical), CPT 1100F (falls risk screening — preventive measure, not an event).

notes.R29.6: Tendency to fall, not elsewhere classified
notes.S22: Fracture of rib(s), sternum, thoracic spine
notes.S32: Fracture of lumbar spine and pelvis
notes.S42: Fracture of shoulder and upper arm
notes.S62: Fracture at wrist and hand level
notes.S82: Fracture of lower leg, including ankle
notes.Z91.81: EXCLUDED — Personal history of falls (administrative/historical, not an event)
notes.1100F: EXCLUDED — Falls risk screening performed (preventive measure, not a fall event)

how_to_identify_administrative_codes: 1. Review code_analysis_protocol_vs_clinical CSV from research outputs
2. Identify codes with high protocol_pct (>80%) that appear in protocol-like sequences
3. Manually review codes to determine if they are administrative vs. clinical
4. Add confirmed administrative codes to this lookup table
5. Codes should be administrative/billing/scheduling, not clinical diagnoses or treatments
research_guidelines.post_event_leakage: Events occurring on or after target event date are automatically classified as administrative (leakage) and do not need to be in this lookup table.
